steps.title: Compute distance for walking and running
steps.content: Distance equals rate times time. Walking gives $$4 \cdot w = 4w$$ miles and running gives $$7 \cdot r = 7r$$ miles.

steps.title: Write the total-distance equation
steps.content:
The combined total is 18 miles, so $$4w + 7r = 18.$$
  • A) $4w+7r=18$ — matches the distance equation.
  • B) $\frac{1}{4}w+\frac{1}{7}r=18$ — uses reciprocals of the rates, which would give time, not distance.
  • C) $\frac{1}{4}w+\frac{1}{7}r=198$ — wrong coefficients and wrong total.
  • D) $4w+7r=198$ — correct coefficients but wrong total (the total is 18, not 198).
That matches Choice A — $4w+7r=18$.
